claim: Verifier confirms explorer and cross-checker evidence meets all verification criteria: no assumptions presented as facts, all evidence paths and sources are usable, and missing data is properly reflected as blockers rather than hidden.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES defines four evidence scopes: project, external, user, unknown.
evidence.why: Verifies evidence scope contract exists in codebase.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope function classifies evidence by scope with explicit fallback logic.
projectEvidenceBlockingIssues generates blockers when project evidence lacks required path locator.
evidence.why: Verifies enforcement of project evidence requirements.

evidence.summary: Test validates PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker for external-only evidence.
evidence.why: Verifies test coverage for scope enforcement.

evidence.summary: Test validates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker when project files are modified.
Doctrine explicitly states project evidence requirements with Chinese documentation.
Both agents have empty assumptions and missing arrays, all 22 records have concrete paths and line numbers.
evidence.why: Verifies no assumptions are presented as facts.

risks: If evidence scope classification has edge-case bugs, external evidence could be accepted as project facts.
If boundary snapshot comparison misses file changes in non-git repos, project mutations could go undetected.
If agent prompts do not enforce scope requirements across providers, agents may return malformed evidence.

result.notes: Verification complete. Explorer and cross-checker both returned 21-22 evidence records with zero assumptions, zero missing data, and all records have concrete repo-relative paths with line numbers. All evidence sources are properly categorized as project_code, project_test, or project_docs. Both agents independently verified the same implementation details and reached identical conclusions (pass, proceed). No conflicts found between agents. All three verification criteria passed: (1) no assumptions presented as facts, (2) all evidence paths/sources are usable, (3) missing data properly reflected in blockers. PRD readiness evidence is sufficient.
